feedback.text: Hello, I want to add my voice to the millions who suffered the loss of content and access to games that were bought that had the “promise” to be unlimited. I will set up a list of games I have bought that are no longer available at installation (degradation of the CD over time, disconnection from a server, deleted from a digital store), the date on which they were offered for sale, the official date of their ‘disconnection’ or ‘killing’ making it unfit for purpose. — Star Wars Galaxies/early June 2003; end Dec 2025 – Battlefield 2142/Début Oct 2006; end June 2014 – Battlefield 2/Juin 2005; end June 2014 – Battlefield 3/Oct 2011; fin Nov 2024 – Medal of Honor Allied Assault/Jan 2002; end October 2010 – Heroes & Generals/Oct 2016; end March 2023 – Crysis/Start Sept 2008; end June 2014 – Crysis 2/Début March 2011; end June 2014 – Star Wars Battlefront (2015)/Nov 2015; fin – [majority of inaccessible content] – Star Wars Battlefront 2 (2017)/Nov 2017; fin – [majority of inaccessible content] – Assistant’s Creed Brotherhood/Nov 2010; Jan 2024 – City of Heroes/April 2004’; fin Nov 2012 – Tabula Rasa/Nov 2007; end of Fev. 2009 – Age of Conan/May 2008; end Jan 2013 – Battlestar Galactica Online/Fev. 2011; end of Fev. 2019 – Hellgate: London/Oct. 2007; Jan. 2009 – Marvel Avengers Alliance/March 2012; end Sept 2016 – Marvel Heroes/2013; fin Nov. 2017 This list may not be complete but it itself represents a significant investment of money. The opportunities to maintain games are the “goodwill” of distribution companies, which does not give legitimate means to those who have paid an estimated fair value to continue playing, if only to show the new generations the games that it played before their birth. The only products that are free of this kind of manipulation are console games cassettes of the 90s and 80s. Everything we want is to be able to preserve our right to keep the games we have paid for. Thank you.
